title: chamaesyce velleriflora
intro: Chamaesyce velleriflora, also known as hairy-seed spurge or hairy-seed milkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is a succulent plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
